seo.title: About the Group — Characom Group
seo.description: The story of Characom Group: founded in Nicosia in 1987, now 650+ engineers and builders delivering infrastructure, developments and homes across Cyprus.

props.heading: Built to endure
props.subheading: One family firm, four decades, three sectors — and a single standard across all of them.

image.alt: Cityscape of completed development at dusk

props.heading: From two crews to six hundred and fifty
props.body:
Characom was founded in 1987 by Andreas Characos, a site engineer who believed a contractor's reputation should be auditable — every promise written down, every handover walked with the client. The first office was a site cabin; the first ledger is still in the boardroom.

Growth came the unfashionable way: no acquisitions, no borrowed badges. The group added a precast yard when its bridges needed one, a joinery workshop when its houses deserved better stairs, and a development arm when clients kept asking who they should buy land with.

The result is unusual on the island — a group that can take a project from first survey to final key under one name, and answer for all of it. That is what we mean when we say we build what outlasts us.

milestones.title: Founded in Nicosia
milestones.text: Andreas Characos registers the firm with two crews and one mixer truck. The first contract: foundations for a neighbour's bakery, finished three days early.

milestones.title: Limassol office opens
milestones.text: The group follows its clients south, opening a second office to serve the coast's growing residential demand.

milestones.title: First government contract
milestones.text: A district school extension in Larnaca begins the public-works record — delivered early, and the start of 85 government contracts to date.

milestones.title: 500th home delivered
milestones.text: The Elaia courtyard scheme in Limassol marks half a thousand families housed. The owners still harvest the development's olive trees every November.

milestones.title: Triple ISO certification
milestones.text: Quality, environment and safety systems certified to ISO 9001, 14001 and 45001 — turning house rules into audited commitments.

milestones.title: The waterfront decade
milestones.text: With the Paphos Harbour Quarter and the Larnaca coastal works both under way, the group's largest chapter is being written at the water's edge.

props.heading: The people who answer for it

members.name: Andreas Characos

photo.alt: Portrait of Andreas Characos, Chairman and Founder

members.bio: Site engineer turned founder. Still walks a site every week, and still keeps the 1987 ledger in the boardroom as a reminder of what a promise costs.

members.name: Elena Characou

photo.alt: Portrait of Elena Characou, Chief Executive Officer

members.bio: Led the group's move into development and its waterfront programme. Believes a builder's best marketing is a client who comes back twice.

members.name: Marios Ioannou

photo.alt: Portrait of Marios Ioannou, Director of Engineering

members.bio: Bridge engineer by training, runs the precast yard and every structural sign-off. His rule for the drawing office: if you would not stand under it, do not stamp it.

members.name: Katerina Georgiou

photo.alt: Portrait of Katerina Georgiou, Director of Quality and Sustainability

members.bio: Keeps the group's three ISO systems and its recycled-materials programme honest. Numbers our clients can audit, not adjectives.

props.heading: Build something that outlasts us both
props.text: From public tenders to private homes — start the conversation.
